SEARCH
Package view

Class GamePanel

java.lang.Object
java.awt.Component
java.awt.Container
javax.swing.JComponent
javax.swing.JPanel
view.BasePanel
view.GamePanel
All Implemented Interfaces:
ImageObserver, MenuContainer, Serializable, Accessible
public class GamePanel extends BasePanel
GamePanel class represents the main game panel where the gameplay occurs. It contains components for displaying the dealer, human player, opponents, and action buttons.
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

  • Constructor Details

    • GamePanel

      private GamePanel()
      Private constructor for GamePanel. Initializes the panel layout and components.

  • Method Details

    • updateBet

      public void updateBet(String name)
      Updates the bet amount and money for a specific player.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the player whose bet and money are to be updated.

    • updateFirstBets

      public void updateFirstBets()
      Updates the initial bets for all players including the human player. Uses a timer to update each player's bet and money labels over time.

    • updateCards

      public void updateCards()
      Updates the cards for players and the dealer. Manages the addition of cards to player and dealer panels based on their current state.

    • updateFirstCards

      public void updateFirstCards()
      Updates the initial cards for players and the dealer. Manages the addition of initial cards to player and dealer panels based on their current state.

    • updateHpCard

      public void updateHpCard()
      Updates the visual representation of the human player's card on the GUI. This method adds the last card from the human player's hand to the specified card panel.

    • turnSecondCard

      public void turnSecondCard()
      Turns the dealer's second card face-up on the GUI. Sets the image of the dealer's second card to the specified button component.

    • updateGame

      public void updateGame(String message)
      Updates the game state based on the provided message indicating win or loss. Displays a message indicating whether the player won or lost, plays corresponding audio, updates player and opponents' statistics, updates UI elements, and resets game visuals.
      Parameters:
      message - "true" if the player won, "false" if the player lost.

    • checkBets

      private int checkBets()
      Checks the total sum of possible human player bets
      Returns:
      The total sum of all bets.

    • removeCards

      public void removeCards()
      Removes all cards from player and dealer panels and resets the game interface. Enables betting actions and updates the request message based on the current betting status. Plays the game over sound if the human player has run out of money

    • setCounters

      public void setCounters()
      Sets counters for the human player based on the current game state. Updates played, level, won, and lost counters.

    • setImageAvatar

      private void setImageAvatar(String path, JButton avatarButton)
      Sets the image of an avatar button using the provided image path.
      Parameters:
      path - The file path of the avatar image.
      avatarButton - The JButton component representing the avatar.

    • setImageCard

      private void setImageCard(String path, JButton card)
      Sets the image of a card button using the provided image path.
      Parameters:
      path - The file path of the card image.
      card - The JButton component representing the card.

    • setChips

      private void setChips(String path, JButton chips)
      Sets the image of chips on a button using the provided image path.
      Parameters:
      path - The file path of the chips image.
      chips - The JButton component representing the chips.

    • setAvatarAndLabels

      public void setAvatarAndLabels()
      Sets the avatar image and labels for all players in the game panel. Updates avatar images, player names, and money labels based on opponent list size.

    • setPlayersInvisible

      public void setPlayersInvisible(int numberPlayer)
      Sets specified player panels invisible based on the provided player number.
      Parameters:
      numberPlayer - The player number indicating which panels to hide.

    • setPlayersVisible

      public void setPlayersVisible()
      Sets all player panels visible.

    • setBets

      public SpinnerListModel setBets()
      Sets up a SpinnerListModel for bets based on possible bet amounts.
      Returns:
      The SpinnerListModel containing possible bet amounts.

    • addBetPanel

      public void addBetPanel(JPanel card, String bet, JLabel LabelBet)
      Adds a bet panel to a card panel displaying the specified bet amount.
      Parameters:
      card - The JPanel where the bet panel will be added.
      bet - The string representation of the bet amount.
      LabelBet - The JLabel component displaying the bet amount.

    • addCard

      public void addCard(JPanel panel, JButton card)
      Adds a card button to a panel displaying the specified card.
      Parameters:
      panel - The JPanel where the card button will be added.
      card - The JButton representing the card.

    • createBetPanel

      public JPanel createBetPanel(JLabel betAmount, String bet)
      Creates a bet panel displaying the bet amount and chips.
      Parameters:
      betAmount - The JLabel displaying the bet amount.
      bet - The string representation of the bet amount.
      Returns:
      The JPanel representing the bet panel.

    • addLabelsPanel

      private JPanel addLabelsPanel(JLabel labelName, JLabel labelMoney)
      Creates a panel displaying player labels for name and money.
      Parameters:
      labelName - The JLabel displaying the player name.
      labelMoney - The JLabel displaying the player money.
      Returns:
      The JPanel representing the labels panel.

    • createCardsPanel

      private JPanel createCardsPanel(JPanel panel1, JPanel panel2)
      Creates a panel displaying two card panels vertically arranged.
      Parameters:
      panel1 - The first JPanel to be displayed.
      panel2 - The second JPanel to be displayed.
      Returns:
      The JPanel representing the cards panel.

    • createCard

      public JButton createCard(String name)
      Creates a card button with the specified card name.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the card to be created.
      Returns:
      The JButton representing the card.

    • createAvatar

      private JButton createAvatar()
      Creates an avatar button with default settings.
      Returns:
      The JButton representing the avatar.

    • createAvatarPanel

      private JPanel createAvatarPanel(JButton avatarButton)
      Creates an avatar panel with the specified avatar button.
      Parameters:
      avatarButton - The JButton representing the avatar.
      Returns:
      The JPanel representing the avatar panel.

    • getInstance

      public static GamePanel getInstance()
      Retrieves the singleton instance of GamePanel. If the instance is null, creates a new instance.
      Returns:
      The singleton instance of GamePanel.

    • getSaveNumber

      public int getSaveNumber()
      Retrieves the save number associated with the game.
      Returns:
      The save number.

    • setSaveNumber

      public void setSaveNumber(int saveNumber)
      Sets the save number for the game.
      Parameters:
      saveNumber - The save number to set.

    • getHumanPlayer

      public Player getHumanPlayer()
      Retrieves the human player object.
      Returns:
      The human player object.

    • setHumanPlayer

      public void setHumanPlayer(Player humanPlayer)
      Sets the human player object.
      Parameters:
      humanPlayer - The human player object to set.

    • getOpponents

      public List<Player> getOpponents()
      Retrieves the list of opponents.
      Returns:
      The list of opponents.

    • setOpponents

      public void setOpponents(List<Player> opponents)
      Sets the list of opponents.
      Parameters:
      opponents - The list of opponents to set.

    • getDealer

      public Player getDealer()
      Retrieves the dealer player object.
      Returns:
      The dealer player object.

    • setDealer

      Sets the dealer player object.
      Parameters:
      dealer - The dealer player object to set.

      Returns:

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides the toString method to provide a string representation of GamePanel.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Component
      Returns:
      A string representation of GamePanel including human player and opponents.

    • paintComponent

      public void paintComponent(Graphics g)
      Overrides the paintComponent method to draw the background image.
      Overrides:
      paintComponent in class JComponent
      Parameters:
      g - The Graphics object to paint.
